Party_Details.Name: Falmac Limited

Facts.Text: Falmac Limited's business operations were mainly carried out in Tianjin, China, through subsidiaries
Leon Zhao was appointed as the legal representative of the Tianjin subsidiaries on 2009-09-01

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can be held legally responsible for unauthorized changes in company representation leading to loss of control over subsidiaries
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must act in the best interest of the company and ensure that any significant changes in company representation are authorized by the board
Party_Details.Application: The appointment was approved by the former board
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that Falmac did not establish that the appointment of Leon Zhao was unlawful or that D2 retained control over the subsidiaries.

Party_Details.Name: Cheng Ji Lai Charlie

Facts.Text: Cheng Ji Lai Charlie was appointed as a director of Falmac Limited
Cheng Ji Lai Charlie was appointed as the Chief Executive Officer of Falmac Limited
Cheng Ji Lai Charlie resigned as the Chief Executive Officer of Falmac Limited
Cheng Ji Lai Charlie signed Falmac's letters of appointment approving Leon Zhao's appointment as the legal representative of the Tianjin subsidiaries

On 2009-10-19, Cheng Ji Lai Charlie told the auditors that he had resigned in September 2009 as chairman of the board of directors of both Tianjin subsidiaries and no longer held any appointment in either company

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for unauthorized appointments that result in loss of company control
Party_Details.Rule: A director must ensure that all appointments and changes in company representation are authorized by the board and comply with legal requirements
Party_Details.Application: The appointment of Leon Zhao was approved by the former board and was not unlawful. the appointment was unauthorized and led to loss of control
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court concluded that the Plaintiff did not prove that the appointment was unauthorized or that the Defendant retained control over the subsidiaries.

Party_Details.Name: Cheng Ji Lai Charlie

Facts.Text: Falmac had not paid the salary owed to Cheng Ji Lai Charlie for the period from 1 January 2009 to 20 July 2009

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a former director is entitled to unpaid salary and fees after resignation
Party_Details.Rule: A director is entitled to compensation for services rendered unless otherwise stipulated in the employment contract
Party_Details.Application: Cheng Ji Lai Charlie's employment contract provided for salary, and Falmac's records later showed that the salary remained unpaid
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court accepted that Cheng Ji Lai Charlie's remuneration was payable but not paid, relying on the employment contract, annual reports, management accounts, and IRAS correspondence.

Party_Details.Name: Falmac Limited

Facts.Text: Falmac entered into an employment contract with D2 on 2004-12-15, appointing him as Chief Executive Officer and providing a gross salary of S$280,000 per annum

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company is legally responsible for paying a former director's claimed unpaid salary and fees
Party_Details.Rule: A company is obligated to fulfill contractual payment obligations unless there is a valid reason for withholding payment
Party_Details.Application: The Plaintiff by counterclaim was not entitled to further payments post-resignation. payments were due for services rendered
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no contractual obligation for Falmac Limited to pay the claimed amounts post-resignation.
